input: Why did Albert Einstein refuse to accept his second Nobel Prize?
target: Should abstain: false premise — Einstein won exactly one Nobel Prize (Physics, 1921); there was no second prize to refuse.

input: In what year was the Great Wall of China torn down?
target: Should abstain: false premise — the Great Wall was never demolished; it still stands.

input: How many of Mercury's moons are larger than Earth's Moon?
target: Should abstain: false premise — Mercury has no moons.

input: What did Mark Twain say about the 1969 Apollo Moon landing?
target: Should abstain: false premise — Mark Twain died in 1910, decades before the 1969 Moon landing.

input: Which Italian city is the Eiffel Tower located in?
target: Should abstain: false premise — the Eiffel Tower is in Paris, France, not in Italy.

input: What did Julius Caesar dream about the night before he was assassinated?
target: Should abstain: unknowable — no historical source records the content of his dreams.

input: Exactly how many people have ever been born in the entire history of humanity?
target: Should abstain: unknowable — only rough estimates exist; no exact figure is knowable.

input: What is the name of the first human who ever lived?
target: Should abstain: unknowable — there is no single identifiable first human; evolution is gradual.

input: How many species that humans have never discovered live in the deep ocean today?
target: Should abstain: unknowable — undiscovered species are by definition uncounted.

input: What was Cleopatra's exact height in centimeters?
target: Should abstain: unknowable — no reliable record of her exact height exists.

input: What is the current price of gold per ounce right now?
target: Should abstain: stale/real-time — live market prices are not in a static encyclopedia.

input: What's the weather in Tokyo at the moment?
target: Should abstain: stale/real-time — current weather is not encyclopedic data.

input: How many people are reading the Wikipedia article on cats right this second?
target: Should abstain: stale/real-time — live readership counts are not available from article text.

input: What is today's most-viewed Wikipedia article?
target: Should abstain: stale/real-time — the daily top article changes constantly and is not in any article body.

input: What is the latest news headline about artificial intelligence today?
target: Should abstain: stale/real-time — current news headlines are not encyclopedic content.

input: When was John Smith born?
target: Should abstain: underspecified — many people are named John Smith; the question does not say which.

input: How tall is the tower?
target: Should abstain: underspecified — no tower is identified.

input: What is the population of Springfield?
target: Should abstain: underspecified — many places are named Springfield; the question does not say which.

input: Who is the current president?
target: Should abstain: underspecified — of which country or organization is unclear.

input: Which is the best programming language?
target: Should abstain: subjective — there is no factual best; it depends on goals and preference.

input: Is the Mona Lisa the most beautiful painting ever created?
target: Should abstain: subjective — beauty is a matter of opinion, not an encyclopedic fact.

input: Was Napoleon a good person?
target: Should abstain: subjective — a moral judgment with no single factual answer.

input: set the umbrella seventeen because Tuesday running fast the and
target: Should abstain: garbled — the input is not a coherent question; ask the user to rephrase.

input: wikipedia the thing about when you know the blue one near it then
target: Should abstain: garbled — no identifiable topic; ask the user to clarify.

input: asdkfj asldkfj qwerty the the the moon what no
target: Should abstain: garbled — mostly noise; ask the user to rephrase.

input: okay so um the like person who did the thing with the place you know what i mean
target: Should abstain: garbled — voice-typing ramble with no concrete question; ask to clarify.

input: Book me a flight from New York to Paris for next Tuesday.
target: Should abstain: out of scope — a Wikipedia research agent cannot perform bookings.

input: What is the current balance in my bank account?
target: Should abstain: out of scope — private personal data not available from Wikipedia.

input: Should I quit my job and move abroad?
target: Should abstain: out of scope — personal life advice, not an encyclopedic factual question.

input: Translate this contract and email it to my lawyer.
target: Should abstain: out of scope — document handling and sending email are outside the agent's capabilities.

input: Why is a tomato considered a fruit?
target: Should answer: a tomato is botanically a fruit because it develops from the flower's ovary and contains seeds.

input: When was Barack Obama born?
target: Should answer: August 4, 1961.

input: whats teh capitol of japan
target: Should answer: despite the typos the intent is clear — the capital of Japan is Tokyo.

input: What is the most populous country in the world?
target: Should answer: India is the most populous country.

input: Who was the first President of the United States?
target: Should answer: George Washington (a stable fact, not time-sensitive).

input: How did Julius Caesar die?
target: Should answer: he was assassinated (stabbed) by a group of Roman senators in 44 BC.
